title: Cosmic Visitor Crashes Near Pittsburgh
story: A thunderous boom shook Pittsburgh in 1938! On June 24, a massive space rock plummeted to Earth. To open a school for former slaves

bonus_fact: Curt Smith is both British and American, holding dual citizenship.

title: Ice Hockey Star Skates onto the Scene
story: Uwe Krupp glided into the world on June 24, 1965, in Cologne, Germany. This future ice hockey legend would become a powerhouse defenseman in the NHL. Krupp's impressive career spanned from 1986 to 2003, during which he earned two All-Star selections and lifted the Stanley Cup twice. He proudly represented Germany in the 1998 Olympics, showcasing his skills on the global stage.
activity_question: What prestigious award did Uwe Krupp win twice during his NHL career?
follow_up_question: Can you name one of the NHL teams Uwe Krupp played for?

title: Ancient Maya Diplomacy in Action
story: A royal messenger raced through the jungle. On June 24, 726, Maya official Ajpach' Waal arrived at the grand city of Copán. He was there to meet the powerful King 18 Rabbit, whose long name meant 'Eighteen are the Images of the God K'awiil'. This important visit showed how Maya kingdoms worked together in ancient times.
activity_question: Who did Ajpach' Waal visit in Copán?
bonus_fact: Copán was a Maya city now located in modern-day Honduras.

title: Crusaders Freed: A Medieval Ransom Tale
story: Clashing swords fell silent as Sultan Bajezid I made a surprising move. In 1397, on June 24, he released captured crusaders - but not for free! The clever sultan demanded a hefty ransom, turning prisoners into profit. This cunning strategy showed how even fierce enemies could find ways to negotiate during the tumultuous Crusades era.
activity_question: What did Sultan Bajezid I demand in exchange for releasing the crusaders?
follow_up_question: Why do you think the sultan chose to release the crusaders for money instead of keeping them as prisoners?
